summary: Resolves concept-level truths into 8 concrete decision domains (parsing, identity, projection, layering, mutation, conflicts, session, rendering) with precise behavioral rules. Causal chains fully cover the edit→validate→accept/reject→mutate/conflict cycle and all three conflict resolution paths. Declared bindings to Contracts and CodeArchitecture are not exercised in the file body, creating a minor verifiability gap.
strengths: Each Decision block is self-contained, semantically dense, and leaves minimal interpretive slack — e.g. 'Graph mutation intent is the only client write primitive' and stable ID precedence ordering are immediately actionable.
Conflict resolution is exhaustively enumerated with distinct when-chains for reload, discard, and manual edit, matching the scenario layer's ResolveConflictScenario without duplicating it.

Clean layer separation: resolves exactly what the concept left open (parsing technology, stable ID derivation rules, optimistic update scope, heartbeat protocol) without restating concept-level truths.
Derivation chains (GraphSystem.derives) mirror and extend the concept's derivation topology, adding PatchPlan and SourceMutation as intermediate artifacts that the concept abstracted away.
The when-chain for ambiguous source rewrite targets closes a gap the concept only hinted at via Truth.surfacedConflicts, demonstrating correct downward resolution.
weaknesses: Contracts blueprint (AgentishGraphContracts) is declared as a binding target but never exercised in the file body, so a reader cannot verify whether contract obligations are fully covered.
CodeArchitecture is declared in the header (realizedBy: CodeArchitecture) but has no corresponding behavioral statements, leaving the code-layer binding purely nominal.
Decision.parsing introduces a concrete technology choice (TypeScript compiler API) that may age faster than the rest of the behavioral model — no fallback or abstraction boundary is stated.
The external-change detection when-chain preserves 'selection and viewport when safe' without defining what 'safe' means at the operational level, deferring a decision that this layer should resolve.
